title: HHS Secretary Notification — 500+ Individuals Threshold Watch

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security §164.408 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.408 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.46: HHS Secretary Notification — 500+ Individuals Threshold Watch.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.46' running on index=privacy sourcetype=hipaa:breach_subject (incident_id, subject_token, included_in_count), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_subject" earliest=-365d
| where included_in_count="true"
| stats dc(subject_token) as affected by incident_id
| eval hhs_required=if(affected>=500,"Yes","No")
| join incident_id [
    search index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:hhs_filing" earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(filed_dttm) as hhs_filed by incident_id ]
| where hhs_required="Yes" AND isnull(hhs_filed)
| table incident_id, affected, hhs_required
description: Rolling count of affected individuals per incident versus the 500-person statutory threshold for timely HHS portal submission.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes privacy and sourcetypes hipaa:breach_subject, hipaa:breach_subject`, hipaa:hhs_filing. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Dedupe subjects carefully per legal guidance; (2) separate incidents vs linked incidents; (3) alert at 450 rolling to create runway. Indexes required: `index=privacy`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_subject`, `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_subject``, `sourcetype=hipaa:hhs_filing`.
visualization: Single value (open >500 without filing), Table (incident, affected).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for rolling count of affected individuals per incident versus the 500-person statutory threshold for timely hhs portal submission. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_subject` (`incident_id`, `subject_token`, `included_in_count`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Dedupe subjects carefully per legal guidance; (2) separate incidents vs linked incidents; (3) alert at 450 rolling to create runway.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_subject" earliest=-365d
| where included_in_count="true"
| stats dc(subject_token) as affected by incident_id
| eval hhs_required=if(affected>=500,"Yes","No")
| join incident_id [
    search index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:hhs_filing" earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(filed_dttm) as hhs_filed by incident_id ]
| where hhs_required="Yes" AND isnull(hhs_filed)
| table incident_id, affected, hhs_required
```

Understanding this SPL

**HHS Secretary Notification — 500+ Individuals Threshold Watch (§164.408)** — Rolling count of affected individuals per incident versus the 500-person statutory threshold for timely HHS portal submission.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_subject` (`incident_id`, `subject_token`, `included_in_count`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: hipaa:breach_subject. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="hipaa:breach_subject", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where included_in_count="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **incident_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hhs_required** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where hhs_required="Yes" AND isnull(hhs_filed)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: ], affected, hhs_filed, hhs_required, incident_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (open >500 without filing), Table (incident, affected).
